quote: Calls for a NeuroAI research program and proposes an 'embodied Turing test' benchmarking AI against animal sensorimotor abilities rather than human language.
Reviews bidirectional exchange between neuroscience and AI; argues both fields benefit from continued interaction (attention, episodic memory, continual learning examples).
Proposes analyzing brains and ANNs along three components: objective functions, learning rules, and architectures.
Distinguishes three levels of analysis: computational, algorithmic/representational, and implementational.
Argues goal-driven (task-optimized) deep networks are the leading framework for predicting and understanding sensory cortical representations.
Frames artificial neural networks as models of brain function within a Lakatosian research programme, with a hard core and testable auxiliary hypotheses.
A sparse coding objective on natural images produces localized, oriented, bandpass receptive fields resembling V1 simple cells.
A hierarchical predictive-coding model in which feedback carries predictions and feedforward carries residual errors reproduces extra-classical receptive-field effects such as end-stopping.
Proposes the free-energy principle: the brain minimizes a variational free-energy bound, unifying perception, action, and learning.
States the Bayesian-coding hypothesis: neural computation represents and manipulates probability distributions to handle uncertainty.
Argues divisive normalization is a canonical neural computation recurring across brain regions, sensory modalities, and species.
Reviews the dynamical-systems / population-level framework: neural computation is understood through the low-dimensional dynamics of population activity (neural manifolds).
A recurrent network of binary units with symmetric weights has stable attractor states usable as content-addressable associative memory.
The Neocognitron is a hierarchical multilayer network of S-cells and C-cells achieving position-invariant pattern recognition, inspired by Hubel-Wiesel simple/complex cells.
Gradient-based learning with convolution and subsampling (LeNet-5) recognizes handwritten digits end-to-end via backpropagation.
A feedforward HMAX-style model with alternating template-matching and max-pooling layers performs robust object recognition comparable to human rapid categorization.
The Differentiable Neural Computer couples a neural network controller to an external memory matrix with differentiable read/write attention, solving algorithmic and graph tasks.
A 'World Model' (VAE + recurrent dynamics) learns a compressed internal model of the environment; agents can train inside the model's hallucinated rollouts.
Grid-like representations emerge in a recurrent network trained to path-integrate, and support vector-based navigation in a deep RL agent.
A predictive-coding network with local Hebbian plasticity can approximate the backpropagation algorithm's weight updates.
Fixed random feedback weights (feedback alignment) can deliver useful learning signals, showing exact symmetric weight transport is not required for error-driven learning.
Midbrain dopamine neurons fire in a pattern matching a temporal-difference reward-prediction error.
A recurrent network trained by dopamine-like RL gives rise to a second, faster reinforcement-learning process in its activity dynamics — casting prefrontal cortex as a meta-RL system.
Complementary Learning Systems theory: a fast hippocampal system and a slow neocortical system; interleaved replay mitigates catastrophic interference — a basis for experience replay.
Proposes the NGRAD hypothesis: the brain may approximate backpropagation-like credit assignment using differences in neural activity driven by feedback.
Surrogate-gradient methods train spiking neural networks by replacing the non-differentiable spike with a smooth surrogate during backpropagation.
Loihi is a 128-core neuromorphic processor implementing spiking neurons with on-chip programmable synaptic learning rules.
TrueNorth integrates 1 million spiking neurons and 256 million synapses, consuming about 70 mW in real-time operation.
SpiNNaker is a massively parallel ARM-core machine designed for real-time simulation of large-scale spiking neural networks.
A time-to-first-spike deep SNN matches ANN accuracy while using on the order of 0.3 spikes per neuron, demonstrating extreme sparsity.
Performance-optimized hierarchical convolutional models, optimized for object recognition, predict neural responses in macaque V4 and IT better than prior models.
Across 37 models compared by representational similarity, only deep supervised networks closely matched inferior temporal cortex representational geometry; unsupervised models did not.
A task-optimized deep network predicts human auditory cortical responses and reveals a hierarchy from primary to non-primary auditory cortex.
Representational similarity analysis (RSA) compares representations via representational dissimilarity matrices, enabling model-to-brain comparison independent of feature spaces.
Across >40 language models, a model's next-word-prediction performance predicts how well it fits human neural and behavioral language responses; the best models approach noise-ceiling fit.
Human (ECoG) and autoregressive deep language models share computational principles: spontaneous pre-onset next-word prediction and contextual embedding geometry.
Brain (fMRI/MEG) and transformer language-model representations partially converge; convergence increases with the model's word-prediction accuracy and depth.
Brain-Score is an integrative benchmark aggregating neural (V1/V2/V4/IT) and behavioral metrics to score how brain-like a model is.
A trained recurrent network reproduces the population dynamics of prefrontal cortex during context-dependent decision-making; selection arises from recurrent dynamics, not input gating.
LFADS, a sequential variational autoencoder, infers single-trial neural population dynamics and yields high behavioral decoding accuracy from motor cortex.
CEBRA learns consistent, behavior- and time-aligned latent embeddings of neural data via a contrastive objective, validated across visual cortex, hippocampus, and motor reach data.
DeepLabCut achieves near human-level markerless pose tracking from a few hundred labeled frames via transfer learning on a pretrained deep network.
Flood-filling networks (recurrent 3D CNNs) perform automated neuron reconstruction in electron-microscopy connectomics with long error-free path lengths.
POYO is a transformer pretrained across many neural recording sessions that tokenizes individual spikes and transfers to new sessions with few-shot decoding.
Networks whose connectivity is fixed to the fly connectome, with only dynamics optimized, predict neural activity across the Drosophila visual system at single-neuron resolution.
A foundation model ('digital twin') of mouse visual cortex trained on large neural recordings generalizes to predict responses to novel stimulus types.
Manifold-capacity theory characterizes object manifolds by geometric measures (manifold radius, dimension) that determine linear separability.

Articulates the 'circuits' programme for mechanistic interpretability: features and weight-defined circuits inside neural networks can be reverse-engineered, framed as a neuroscience of artificial networks.
Bayesian Program Learning represents concepts as probabilistic programs, learning to recognize and generate handwritten characters from a single example and passing a visual Turing test.
Argues human-like machines need intuitive physics, intuitive psychology, compositionality, causality, and learning-to-learn — not just pattern recognition.
Introduces SCAN and shows standard seq2seq recurrent networks fail at systematic compositional generalization to novel combinations.
Meta-learning for compositionality (MLC) gives a standard neural network human-level systematic generalization, matching human error patterns.
An 'intuitive physics engine' based on probabilistic simulation predicts human judgments about physical scenes such as tower stability.
Defines intelligence as skill-acquisition efficiency and introduces the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) as a benchmark resistant to memorization.
ACT-R is a unified cognitive architecture with modular components (declarative/procedural memory, perceptual-motor modules) mappable to brain regions.
Proposes a 'global latent workspace' to bring Global Workspace Theory of consciousness into deep-learning architectures coordinating specialist modules.
An intracortical BCI decoded imagined handwriting into text at about 90 characters per minute with high accuracy in a paralyzed participant.
An intracortical speech neuroprosthesis decoded attempted speech at about 62 words per minute, several times faster than prior records.
An ECoG speech neuroprosthesis decoded attempted speech to text, synthesized voice, and drove a facial avatar at roughly 78 words per minute.
A speech neuroprosthesis achieved high accuracy with a large (125,000-word) vocabulary and was usable from the first day, in an ALS participant.
Seen natural images were reconstructed from fMRI using a latent diffusion (Stable Diffusion) model without fine-tuning the generative model.
MindEye reconstructs and retrieves seen images from fMRI using contrastive learning plus a diffusion prior, on the Natural Scenes Dataset.
A non-invasive (fMRI) decoder reconstructs the semantic gist of perceived and imagined continuous language, leveraging a language model.
A virtual rodent controlled by a deep-RL network performs multiple motor tasks; its artificial neural activity is analyzed with neuroscience methods ('deep neuroethology').
A full-body biomechanical virtual rat trained in simulation produces network activity that predicts real rat neural activity (motor cortex, striatum) better than kinematic variables.
Proposes Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPA) and a modular configurator/actor/critic/world-model design for autonomous machine intelligence.
Argues current deep neural network models of human vision fail key psychophysical and behavioral tests and that benchmark scores like Brain-Score can be confounded.
Lists challenges for deep learning including data hunger, brittleness/poor transfer, opacity, and weak handling of compositional and causal structure.
Argues computational modeling forces theory-building and that much of psychological/cognitive science suffers a theory deficit.
Reviews how large language models are used as models of human language processing in the brain, including encoding models and methodological caveats.
Reviews deep learning as a framework for neuroscience and argues the value lies in asking which computational problem the brain solves, not just fitting activity.
The Tianjic chip is a hybrid architecture supporting both ANN and SNN paradigms on one substrate, demonstrated controlling an autonomous bicycle.
SpikingJelly is an open-source deep-learning framework for spiking neural networks reporting large training-speed improvements and full-stack neuromorphic support.
ImageNet-trained CNNs are biased toward texture rather than shape, unlike humans; training on stylized images increases shape bias and improves robustness.
BrainCog is an open-source spiking-neural-network platform spanning brain simulation and brain-inspired cognitive functions with multi-scale plasticity rules.
The 2014 demonstration that ImageNet-optimized deep nets predict ventral-stream responses marks the empirical onset of the modern goal-driven NeuroAI program.
The 2023 NeuroAI roadmap consolidated the field's identity and named the embodied Turing test as an organizing goal.
DreamerV3 learns a world model and an actor-critic that plans in imagined latent rollouts, mastering 150+ tasks with fixed hyperparameters and collecting diamonds in Minecraft from scratch.
V-JEPA learns visual representations by predicting masked features in latent space (not pixels), a non-generative video world-model objective.
Genie is a foundation world model that generates action-controllable, playable interactive environments from images or video, learning a latent action space from unlabelled video alone.

The Tolman-Eichenbaum Machine models the hippocampal-entorhinal system as a world model that factorizes relational structure (entorhinal grid-like codes) from sensory content (hippocampal place-like codes), predicting non-random remapping.
The hippocampus is proposed to encode a predictive map (the successor representation) of future states, explaining biased place fields, with grid cells as its low-dimensional eigenvectors.
Slot Attention binds image features into a fixed set of permutation-invariant 'slots' via iterative attention, enabling unsupervised object discovery.

The Neuro-Symbolic Concept Learner jointly learns visual concepts and a semantic program executor over an object-based scene representation, achieving near-perfect, data-efficient CLEVR visual question answering.

A self-supervised video model (V-JEPA) trained only to predict in latent space exhibits violation-of-expectation 'surprise' on intuitive-physics tests (object permanence, continuity), whereas pixel-prediction and multimodal-LLM models remain near chance.
OpenAI frames large video generation models as 'world simulators', the centerpiece of the debate over whether pixel-level video generation constitutes a world model.

The Deep Convolutional Inverse Graphics Network learns a disentangled 'graphics code' (pose, lighting, shape) and re-renders scenes under novel transformations — vision as inverse graphics / analysis-by-synthesis.
Argues relational and combinatorial inductive biases, unified under a general 'graph network' block, are key to human-like generalization.
V-JEPA 2 scales the latent video-prediction objective to ~1B parameters and over a million hours of video, then post-trains on robot trajectories to enable zero-shot planning.

IntPhys 2 is a violation-of-expectation video benchmark (permanence, immutability, spatio-temporal continuity, solidity) on which state-of-the-art models score near chance while humans are near-perfect.
Reviews spike-based machine intelligence and neuromorphic computing, arguing for algorithm-hardware co-design of energy-efficient spiking systems.
The Natural Scenes Dataset is a massive 7-tesla fMRI dataset over tens of thousands of natural scenes, built to bridge cognitive neuroscience and AI.
THINGS-data is a multimodal collection (fMRI, MEG, and 4.7M behavioral similarity judgments) over 1,854 object concepts for studying object representations in brain and behavior.
Critically reviews theories of how the brain might approximate error backpropagation (predictive coding, target propagation, etc.) and assesses where each is biologically plausible or breaks down.
Resource-rational analysis frames human cognition as the optimal use of limited computational resources, unifying rationality with cognitive constraints.
Proposes that reinforcement-learning metaparameters (learning rate, inverse temperature, discount factor) are controlled by neuromodulators (dopamine, serotonin, acetylcholine, noradrenaline).
A contrastive model decodes perceived speech from non-invasive MEG/EEG recordings across 175 subjects, identifying the correct speech segment with up to ~41% top-1 accuracy among >1000 candidates.
Distinguishes two senses of machine consciousness — global availability (C1) and self-monitoring (C2) — within a Global Neuronal Workspace framework, arguing current AI implements neither.

Reviews the balanced spiking-network theory of efficient coding, in which tightly balanced excitation/inhibition implements an efficient predictive code and membrane voltage represents prediction error.
A deep convolutional spiking network trained with unsupervised STDP and temporal (rank-order) coding performs object recognition with one spike per neuron.
Presents practical machine-learning patterns for neuroimaging decoding/encoding with scikit-learn, seeding the nilearn library.

Centaur, a large language model (Llama 3.1 70B) fine-tuned on Psych-101 (160 experiments, 60,092 participants, 10.7M choices), predicts held-out human behavior across many cognitive tasks and generalizes to new ones; its internal representations become more aligned with human neural activity after fine-tuning.

TRIBE is the first non-linear, multi-subject, multimodal (text/audio/video) deep encoder of whole-brain fMRI responses; it won the Algonauts 2025 challenge (1st of 263 teams), with the multimodal advantage largest in associative cortex.
A model decodes images from MEG, reconstructing perceived visual stimuli from temporally-resolved non-invasive brain signals roughly 7x better than linear baselines, toward real-time decoding.
Brain2Qwerty decodes sentences from non-invasive EEG/MEG recorded while participants type, reaching a character error rate around 32% (best 19%) for MEG.

TRIBE v2, a tri-modal (video/audio/language) foundation model trained on over 1,000 hours of fMRI across 720 subjects, predicts high-resolution brain responses to novel stimuli, tasks and subjects, enables in-silico experimentation that recovers seminal visual and neuro-linguistic results, and reveals the topography of multisensory integration; built on the Algonauts-2025-winning TRIBE.
notes: Successor to P102 (TRIBE v1); Meta release with open weights+code (CC BY-NC). Recent 2026 work — venue arXiv/Meta, exact arXiv ID not captured.

quote: Decodes individual words from non-invasive brain recordings (MEG/EEG) across 723 participants, a large-cohort step toward non-invasive word-level decoding.
